title: Why Sleep Locks In Your Memories
script_summary: Uncover how sleep transforms your daily experiences into lasting memories.

segments.voiceover: While you sleep, your brain is filing today's memories for long-term storage.
Sleep isn’t just rest; it's a powerful memory processor. Ever wondered how this works?
During slow-wave sleep, your brain sorts and stabilizes new memories like a librarian organizing books.
Then comes REM sleep, where emotions connected to those memories are intensified, making them more vivid.
So, when you dream, your brain isn’t just goofing off; it's solidifying your experiences for the future.
Want to unlock the secrets of your brain? Like and follow for more fascinating insights!
